hero.subtitle: What Mexico buys — spending categories from government procurement
hero.detail: 72 regex-based categories classify 3.1M contracts (2002-2025) by title with accent-aware matching. Coverage: ~84% specific, ~16% unclassified.
hero.headline: What the Government Buys: A Category Breakdown
hero.analysisSubtitle: Analysis of Partida budget codes revealing where public spending and corruption risk concentrate

treemap.title: Category Treemap by Value
treemap.description: Size represents total contract value. Colors indicate sector classification.
treemap.empty: No category data available yet. Please contact your administrator.
treemap.hint: Size = total value · Color = sector · Click a category to view its profile

table.title: Top Categories
table.empty: No categories found. Category data may not be available yet.
table.noPairData: No vendor-institution data available for this category.
table.noResults: No categories found with current filters.

trends.title: Spending Trends — Top 5 Categories
trends.description: Year-over-year contract value for the five largest spending categories.

errors.checkConnection: Check your connection and try again.

flags.criticalRisk: Critical risk — pattern matches documented corruption cases
flags.highRisk: High risk — review vendor concentration patterns
flags.narrativeHighDA: This category combines {{riskLevel}} risk ({{riskPct}}%) with {{daPct}}% direct award — {{daMultiple}}x the OECD limit of 25%.
flags.narrativeMedRisk: Average {{riskLevel}} risk across {{contracts}} contracts worth {{value}}. Review concentration patterns.
flags.narrativeHighDAOnly: {{daPct}}% direct award — {{daMultiple}}x the OECD limit of 25%. Low competition in this category.

subcategory.title: What's Being Purchased
subcategory.description: {{count}} subcategories · {{name}} · click a row to expand

filters.tableHint: Click any row to view subcategories, top vendors and institutional breakdowns.

riskMap.title: Risk vs. Value: The 72-Category Landscape
riskMap.scatterDescription: Each dot is a category. X axis = total spend 2002–2025. Y axis = average risk. Categories in the upper right require immediate attention.

sexenio.title: Spending by Administration: How Government Purchases Changed
sexenio.description: Cumulative federal spending in the 10 highest-value categories, broken down by presidential administration.
sexenio.barChartHint: Top 30 spending categories by total amount. Color = sector. Click a bar for detail.
sexenio.footnote: Sheinbaum = 2025 partial. Fox includes partial COMPRANET data.

lede.text: Of the {{count}} federal spending categories, those with the highest risk show corruption indicators up to {{pct}}% above average. The breakdown by Partida budget code reveals where spending concentrates and which goods and services show the most suspicious patterns.

scatter.noData: No category data available for the risk quadrant.

impacto.text: Medicine and medical equipment categories represent a substantial share of health sector spending — and show elevated risk indicators.
